meta.name: C. elegans Full Connectome — 302 Neurons, 2 Layers (Varshney 2011)
meta.description: C. elegans connectome extended with a third layer: scientific_attention (d4). d1 = chemical_synapse (Varshney 2011 S/Sp). d2 = gap_junction (Varshney 2011 EJ). d4 = scientific_attention: co-mention of two neurons in the same PubMed paper (query: '"Caenorhabditis elegans"[tiab] AND "[NEURON]"[tiab]'). Edge weight = number of shared PMIDs. This tests the Universal Layer Grammar d4 extension: does hub identity persist into the observer layer? Pre-registration: F12_D4_OBSERVER_v1.
meta.experiment: F12_D4_OBSERVER_v1 — d4 observer layer extension of F12
meta.data_source: White et al. (1986) Phil Trans R Soc Lond B 314:1-340; Varshney et al. (2011) PLoS Comput Biol 7(2):e1001066; Downloaded from https://www.wormatlas.org/images/NeuronConnect.xls
meta.validation_class: F12 — full connectome scale-up of M_EXT2 true external validation

layers.chemical_synapse: S/Sp type entries: directed neurotransmitter release (Neuron1=presynaptic). Weight = number of synaptic contacts.
layers.gap_junction: EJ type entries: undirected electrical coupling. Weight = number of gap junction contacts.
layers.scientific_attention: d4 observer layer: co-mention in PubMed (Caenorhabditis elegans context). Two neurons are connected if they co-appear in ≥1 PubMed title/abstract. Weight = number of shared PMIDs. Built from PubMed Entrez API, 2026-06-13.

meta.hub_seed_source: M_EXT2 (celegans_connectome.json) — 15 command interneurons; pre-registered in celegans_connectome.prediction
